# CRISP Content Engine, extended reference

See https://crisp.you/llms.txt for the primary summary.

## Homepage

CRISP Content Engine helps founders, consultants, agencies and lean teams build consistent visibility without burning out. The homepage explains the content system stack: brand profile, strategy-led generation, content queue, human review, export and auto-publishing paths, AI image prompts and future reporting.

Start free at https://app.crispdigital.io with no credit card required.

## Pricing

- Starter: Free forever, export workflows, manual posting, no credit card
- Creator: $15/month, LinkedIn auto-publish where connected, X export, blog articles
- Growth: $29/month, LinkedIn and Meta auto-publish where connected, higher volume
- Pro: $49/month, up to 3 brands, higher volume
- Scale: Custom limits, seats and onboarding via enquiries@crispdigital.io

Billing: https://app.crispdigital.io/billing

## Features

Brand profile stores tone, audience, offers, keywords, exclusions and content rules.
Content queue keeps drafts, channels, statuses and publishing paths visible.
LinkedIn content system supports strategy-led drafts, review and auto-publishing on supported plans.
AI image prompts provide visual direction matched to post, platform and brand.

## Use cases

Founders: visibility without a second full-time content job.
Consultants: authority content connected to expertise and offer clarity.
Agencies: multi-brand workflows with separate profiles and queues.
Lean teams: shared queue, approvals and channel outputs.

## Comparisons

ChatGPT vs CRISP: prompt-first drafting vs brand-profile-first workflow with queue and publishing paths.
Content calendar vs content engine: scheduling visibility vs connected strategy, drafts, review and publishing.

## Articles summary

Founders need a system that connects brand, themes, channels, review and publishing rhythm.
AI generators help isolated drafts; content systems help consistency across weeks and channels.
LinkedIn consistency requires workflow, not willpower alone.
Businesses need brand context, queue, review and publishing path connected.
Calendars plan dates; engines create the work behind the plan.
Founders should look for brand memory, review control and a free starting point.
One strong idea can become multiple channel-ready outputs with proper adaptation.
Brand voice should be stored once and reused, not rebuilt every prompt.

## Free entry

https://crisp.you/free-ai-content-planner describes the Starter plan as the free entry into the full CRISP Content Engine workflow.
